title: Sum of all Submatrices
hints: Instead of generating all submatrices, think about the contribution of each element.
For an element at position (i, j), count how many submatrices include this element.
An element at (i, j) will be included in all submatrices that start at any (x, y) where x <= i and y <= j, and end at any (p, q) where p >= i and q >= j.
The number of submatrices containing A[i][j] is (i+1) * (j+1) * (N-i) * (N-j).
Sum up the contribution of each element: A[i][j] * (i+1) * (j+1) * (N-i) * (N-j).
